commit.message:
Improve tab completions grid rendering logic (#365)

Implements four improvements to the tab completion grid rendering:

## Changes Made

- **Early "No suggestions" exit**: When there are zero filtered
suggestions, the "No suggestions" message is printed immediately at the
top of the render block and the grid-building logic is skipped entirely.

- **Middle-ellipsis truncation for wide suggestions**: If a suggestion's
text is wider than the terminal width and it falls in the first visible
column, `render()` now applies middle truncation (e.g.
`very_long_path_name` → `very_l…ame`) instead of silently discarding the
column.

- **2D selected position**: `ActiveSuggestions` now tracks
`(selected_row, selected_col)` instead of a flat index. Up/down arrows
navigate within a column; left/right arrows jump between columns. If
navigating right would land on an empty cell in a partial last column,
the selection wraps back to column 0.

- **Column scroll offset**: A `col_scroll_offset` field ensures the
selected column is always visible. `into_grid()` accepts a `col_offset`
parameter to skip leading columns, and the offset is updated after each
render and eagerly on navigation so there is no visible lag.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Tab completions grid improvements:
> if the number of active suggestions is zero, the print the No
suggestions at the start of the render block and exit that block.
> 
> If the suggestion is too wide to fit onto a single line and we are
drawing the first column, the remove the middle part of the suggestion
and replace it with the ellipses character.
> 
> Keep track of the selected position as a 2d value, not just an index.
When you move to the right, increase the column index. The in the index
check function, if there isn't a suggestion for that 2d pos, set col to
0 etc.
> 
> If the cursor goes too far to the right, we currently wont be able to
draw this column. So we should skip some columns so that the column with
the cursor is always drawn.


</details>

commit.message:
Fix slice assignment invalidation precision

Same-length slice replacement now stamps only the replaced indices
instead of everything from the first replaced index to the end of the
array.  Different-length replacement (which shifts elements) still
bumps from the first affected index onward.

Also fixes a pre-existing bug where pure insertion via empty slice
(e.g. arr[2:2] = [9]) failed to invalidate shifted proxies.

commit.message:
Fix KeysView.__or__/__xor__ to accept any iterable

Python's dict_keys set operations accept any iterable, not just sets.
Our __or__ and __xor__ delegated to set.__or__/set.__xor__ which
reject non-set operands like lists and tuples.

Fix by converting the other operand to a set first via iterable_to_pyset
helper before calling the set method.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor subscript dispatch with SubscriptKey enum

Replace the mixed key/item dispatch in __getitem__, __setitem__, and
__delitem__ with a unified SubscriptKey enum that resolves proxy keys
and classifies key type (Str/Int/Slice/Other) once before borrowing
the document.

- Add SubscriptKey enum and resolve_subscript_key() in item_ops
- Add subscript_type_error() helper for wrong-key-type errors
- Split item_setitem into item_setitem_str/item_setitem_int
- Split item_delitem into item_delitem_str/item_delitem_int
- item_delitem_str delegates to table_pop (no duplication)
- Move table_pop from item_ops to dict_ops (correct module home)
- Remove dead code: item_getitem, item_setitem, item_delitem,
  require_str_key, require_int_key
- Support proxy keys in Document/DictProxy get/getitem/setdefault
- Add extract_key_str for Document string-only key resolution
- Preserve Python dict error semantics (KeyError for dict misses,
  TypeError for wrong key types)

commit.message:
Refactor exit criteria and add weighted circuit support (#185)

* Refactor exit-criteria to nested object format

Change from flat exit_criteria/threshold fields to nested exit-criteria
object with metric and threshold sub-fields. This modernizes the YAML
schema and prepares for weighted circuit support.

- triforce.yaml: all circuit entries use new exit-criteria format
- scenario_wrapper.py: new ExitCriteria Pydantic model, TrainingCircuitEntry
  uses Field(alias='exit-criteria') for kebab-case YAML keys
- train.py: extract metric/threshold from nested ExitCriteria object

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add kind and weight fields to training circuit models

- TrainingCircuitDefinition gets kind field (sequential or weighted)
- TrainingCircuitEntry gets optional weight field
- Validation: weighted circuits require weight on all entries,
  sequential circuits must not have weight
- Existing circuits default to kind=sequential and continue to work

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add WeightedScenarioSelector and switch_scenario support

- WeightedScenarioSelector: centralized class tracking per-scenario step
  counts. Single update(scenario, steps) -> next_scenario method for RPC.
- ScenarioWrapper: switch_scenario() swaps critic, conditions, room
  selector, metrics. reset() integrates with weighted selector when present.
  Step counter tracks steps per episode for selector reporting.
- StateChangeWrapper: switch_scenario() updates objective, per_reset/room/frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add weighted env factory and scenario-aware MetricTracker

- zelda_env.py: make_weighted_zelda_env() creates env stack with
  ScenarioWrapper connected to a WeightedScenarioSelector
- ml_ppo_worker.py: WeightedEnvFactory carries scenario defs, action
  space, and selector proxy for worker subprocesses
- metrics.py: MetricTracker now buffers per-scenario metrics during
  weighted mode scenario switches. get_metrics_and_clear() returns
  {scenario: {metric: value}} in weighted mode, flat dict normally.
- ScenarioWrapper passes scenario_name to MetricTracker in weighted mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add weighted training loop to PPO

- train_weighted(): public entry point for weighted circuit training
- _train_weighted_single(): single-env mode, uses selector directly
- _train_weighted_multi(): multi-env mode, exposes WeightedScenarioSelector
  via multiprocessing.managers.BaseManager for worker RPC access
- _check_weighted_exit_criteria(): per-scenario exit criteria checking
- _average_weighted_metric_dicts(): aggregates per-scenario metrics from
  multiple workers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wire up weighted circuit dispatch in train.py

- _run_circuit() detects weighted circuits and dispatches to
  _run_weighted_circuit(), keeping sequential path as _run_sequential_circuit()
- _run_weighted_circuit() resolves scenario defs, validates, builds exit
  criteria map, and calls ppo.train_weighted()
- _get_kwargs_from_args() now returns circuit_def for kind detection
- main() passes circuit_def through to _run_circuit()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for weighted circuit infrastructure

19 tests covering:
- ExitCriteria Pydantic model parsing (kebab-case alias)
- TrainingCircuitDefinition kind/weight fields and validation
- WeightedScenarioSelector deficit-based scenario selection
- Backward compatibility of existing sequential circuits
- MetricTracker per-scenario buffering in weighted mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add polish weighted circuit to triforce.yaml

Example weighted circuit: 70% full-game, 5% dungeon1, 5% dungeon1-wallmaster,
10% dungeon1-dungeon2. Use for post-training polishing to maintain all skills
while improving weak areas.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix weighted circuit runtime issues and add polish circuit

Fixes:
- on_circuit_start/on_scenario_start name mismatch causing StopIteration
- on_metrics crash with per-scenario nested dicts (flatten for display)
- _aggregate_metrics crash with nested dicts from weighted workers
- network_class duplicate kwarg in train_weighted call
- Test update: polish circuit is now in triforce.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clean up pylint warnings in weighted circuit code

- Add scenario_name property to MetricTracker to avoid protected-access warnings
- Extract _train_weighted_multi_loop helper to reduce nesting depth
- Move imports to correct methods to fix unused-import warnings
- Suppress unavoidable pylint warnings (no-member on dynamic manager, consider-using-with)
- Add too-many-arguments disables on _run_circuit and _run_weighted_circuit
- Remove unused scenario_names variable in train.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KL rollback to prevent catastrophic policy updates

When approx_kl exceeds target_kl * 4 (default: 0.08), the entire
optimization round is discarded and model weights are restored to
their pre-optimization state. This prevents the cascading failure
observed in weighted circuit training where a single bad batch
caused a 10x KL spike, destroying the value function and collapsing
progress from 14 to 0.

The rollback threshold is configurable via kl_rollback multiplier
(default 4.0x target_kl). A losses/kl_rollback metric is logged
to tensorboard for visibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show KL rollback warning in training display

When a KL rollback occurs, displays a red '⚠ KL ROLLBACK - weights restored'
warning in the metrics panel. Only shown when rollback actually fires.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show cumulative KL rollback count in training display

Tracks total rollback count across the run. Displays as a red
'⚠ KL rollbacks: N' line in the metrics panel, only when > 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix attention overlay crash in record.py for MultiHeadAgent

forward_with_attention returns (... spatial_attn, cross_attn).
The code was using result[-1] (cross_attn, 5D) instead of

commit.message:
Preserve array boundary spacing on insert/append/extend

When appending, extending, or inserting at the end of an array, the
old last element's suffix (space before `]`) was left in place,
producing corrupted output like `[1, 2 , 3]`. Fix by stripping the
suffix before mutation and reapplying it to the new last element.

When inserting at index 0, the old first element's prefix (space
after `[`) was lost. Fix by copying it to the new first element;
the old prefix naturally becomes inter-element spacing.

Extract value_prefix/value_suffix helpers to deduplicate the
repeated decor-reading pattern across boundary helpers and
save_removal_decor.

Fix Document.__delitem__/pop double-borrow panic with proxy keys

Both methods used &mut self, causing PyO3 to hold a mutable borrow
while key.extract() ran Python code on proxy keys — triggering a
double-borrow panic. Switch to slf: &Bound<Self> and use
extract_key_str (which safely resolves proxies before any borrow).

Also switch DictProxy.pop to use extract_key_str for consistency.

commit.message:
Replace shift tracking with per-element stamping in MutationTrie

Remove the shifted_from/shifts mechanism from TrieNode entirely. Array
mutations now stamp each affected index individually via stamp_child,
matching the O(n) cost toml_edit already pays to shift elements.

This eliminates two false-staleness bugs:
- stamp_shift min-compression lost precision when two shifts had
  increasing thresholds (min collapsed them)
- stamp_child did not clear shifted_from, so old shift metadata
  poisoned later shifts after an array was replaced

The trie now has just two operations: stamp and stamp_child.
Affected::Shift(usize) becomes Affected::Range { from, to }.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Precise array shift invalidation for list mutations

Add shifted_from field to MutationTrie for range-based proxy
invalidation.  Mid-array insert, remove, pop, and slice operations now
only invalidate proxies at or after the shift point — earlier proxies
survive.

- Add Affected enum (Child vs Shift) replacing the overloaded
  Option<Key> return type from list mutation helpers
- Add Affected::for_removal() constructor to deduplicate the
  end-of-array optimisation across Array and AoT arms
- item_insert returns Option<Affected> for consistency
- stamp_shift on MutationTrie accumulates by taking min(threshold)
- stamp() clears shifted_from since revised_at subsumes it
- Slice deletion uses bump_shift(min_idx); slice assignment uses
  bump_shift(start)
- 7 new trie unit tests, 8 new Python staleness tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add staleness checks to KeysView, ValuesView, ItemsView

Views now store a creation revision and check the mutation trie before
every access, matching the existing ItemProxy staleness model.

Previously, accessing a view whose path had been replaced (e.g.
`view = doc["foo"].keys(); doc["foo"] = "bar"; list(view)`)
raised a confusing TypeError ("string does not support keys()").
Now it raises RuntimeError("stale"), consistent with how proxies
behave.

The staleness check is unified in Document.check_fresh(), used by both
ItemProxy and views, with a single consistent error message.

Root-level views (doc.keys()) remain live for child mutations since
the root path is never invalidated — only doc.clear() makes them stale.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: deduplicate array removal logic in list_ops

item_remove_at is now the single implementation for removing an element
from an array-like TOML item (Array or ArrayOfTables) with correct
decor handling.

- list_pop delegates to item_remove_at after resolving the index.
- item_delitem (Array/AOT branches) delegates via as_array_like_mut,
  collapsing two match arms into one.
- save_removal_decor is called with the correct booleans directly
  instead of saving both decorations then nullifying the unneeded ones.

from the old two-column layout with bare TextBox and small-caps labels
to a clean, flat single-column form layout that fits within the

  dividers between logical groups
- Add StrataIconPicker in a Flyout for emoji selection (Agents, Skills)
  replacing the cramped 72px TextBox

  prevent horizontal overflow of TextBox controls

  Servers, Tools) are visible without scrolling on standard window sizes
- Use localized strings for all field labels
- Keep StrataSetting/StrataSettingGroup only for Type/Enabled toggles in
  MCP Servers where the horizontal label+control layout is appropriate
